name: Vermont Corn Chowder
recipeInstructions:
Use cooked fresh kernels or canned or cooked frozen.
1. Cook the diced salt pork over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until all of the pork pieces become brown but not too dry.
2. Remove the pork with a slotted spoon.
3. Saute the onion in the pork fat just until tender, but not brown.
4. Add water, corn, potatoes, and salt; cook uncovered until the potatoes are almost soft.
5. Add milk and simmer very gently for 10 to 15 minutes to let the flavor mature.
6. Taste and adjust seasoning with freshly ground pepper.
7. Sprinkle the surface with the remaining salt pork and serve with a large supply of soda crackers.
